# TDD Grill Findings

## T1. First RED Must Stay At Public Boundaries

Resolution: Dynamic behavior starts with `run_dual_agent_workflow` or `start_dual_agent_gate`; helper tests for the receipt module are secondary.

## T2. Hash Checks Must Not Depend On External Services

Resolution: Tests create local fixture transcript/output/replay/comparison files and verify sha256 locally.

## T3. Automatic Receipts Must Be Generated By Supervisor Code

Resolution: Tests pass only compact subagent result receipts; the supervisor must synthesize preview-gate receipts before P13.

## T4. Resume Test Must Prove Acceleration

Resolution: Count fake runner calls. Accepted prior gates must not be invoked on rerun.

## T5. SDK Import Test Must Exercise Real Import Behavior

Resolution: Remove `claude_agent_sdk` from `sys.modules`, block import, reload `supervisor.agent_invoker`, and assert class import succeeds.

## T6. Transport Recovery Must Not Be Proven By An Inline Call

Resolution: Add submit/poll tests at the MCP boundary. The submit test proves the tool returns after spawning a detached worker; the poll test proves a durable result can be recovered from disk and state after the initiating call is gone.
